summary: Phase 10d - ARCO Rectificacion-Cancelacion-Oposicion (LFPDPPP). 12/12 backend tests pass for the two new endpoints. Verified: (1) POST /api/users/me/marketing-consent unauth -> 401; opt_out=true persists marketing_opt_out=true + iso marketing_opt_out_at on the user; opt_out=false flips it back and response is exactly {ok:true, marketing_opt_out:false}. (2) DELETE /api/users/me/account unauth -> 401. (3) confirmation != 'ELIMINAR' -> 400 + Spanish detail mentioning 'ELIMINAR' and user NOT flagged deleted. (4) Wrong password -> 401 'Contrasena incorrecta', user not flagged deleted. (5) Role=BUSINESS owner (is_manager=False) -> 400 with 'duen/negocio' message; manager (is_manager=True) IS allowed and gets 200 + account_deleted=true. (6) Active bookings (status in ['confirmed','pending'], n=2) -> 400 with count + 'activa(s)' in detail; user not flagged. (7) wallet.balance_mxn>0 (50.50) -> 400 with the dollar amount in the detail. (8) Happy path: user redacted (email='deleted_<id>@bookvia.deleted', full_name='[Cuenta eliminada]', phone='', photo_url=None, favorites=[], saved_cards=[], active=False, email_verified=False, password_hash rotated and original password no longer verifies via bcrypt), account_deleted=true, account_deleted_at iso, account_deleted_ip captured from first X-Forwarded-For hop ('9.9.9.9'). (9) db.user_favorites and db.notifications for that user_id are fully purged; bookings, payment_transactions, and the user_wallets row are NOT touched. (10) audit_logs row inserted with action='account_deleted_by_user', actor_id==target_id==user_id, ip captured. (11) Email send is best-effort and does not fail the request (Resend MOCKED in environment). (12) Post-delete login with original email -> 401 (user not found by email since email was rewritten); login with redacted deleted_<id>@bookvia.deleted + original password -> 401 (password_hash is hash of generate_id() so original password cannot verify).
critical_code_review_comments: routers/users.py delete_my_account: order of validation is confirmation -> user lookup -> password -> role -> bookings -> wallet. This means a logged-in user can probe other users' password validity? No - find_one is on token_data.user_id, so safe. But the password check happens BEFORE the role-block message: a business OWNER who types the wrong password gets 'Contrasena incorrecta' (401) instead of being told upfront 'owners cannot self-delete'. Reordering role-check before password-check would give clearer UX (and also avoid disclosing whether the password was correct on a path the user cannot complete).

routers/users.py: account_deleted_at is written as ISO string while other audit_logs in the codebase typically use BSON datetime. Same inconsistency we flagged in 10c. Consider datetime.now(timezone.utc) (object) so range queries on the field work.

routers/users.py: redacted_email = f'deleted_{user[id]}@bookvia.deleted'. There is no DB unique index protection if two deletions race for same id (impossible) but the redacted email uses a non-routable TLD - consider documenting that the email-uniqueness index (if any) tolerates the .deleted suffix; otherwise re-deleting an already-soft-deleted account would update an already redacted user fine but a CREATE flow that picks 'deleted_*@bookvia.deleted' as a user-supplied email could collide. Recommend rejecting any registration whose email ends with '.deleted' or '@bookvia.deleted'.

routers/users.py: wallet block uses `balance > 0.0` strictly; negative balances (refunds owed by the user) are NOT blocked. If the platform ever supports negative wallet balance that represents a debt, deleting would erase that debt-tracking. Consider `abs(balance) > 0`.

routers/users.py: business OWNER block message tells user to email soporte@bookvia.app. There is no explicit support-contact endpoint in this flow; ensure that mailbox is monitored or replace with an in-app support form.

routers/users.py: stripe_customer_id is set to None on delete but the actual Stripe customer / saved_cards on Stripe side are NOT cancelled/detached via the Stripe API. PII still lives at Stripe. Consider calling stripe.Customer.delete(stripe_customer_id) or at least documenting the residual data location to satisfy LFPDPPP 'cancelacion' completeness.

routers/users.py: bookings are NOT anonymized at all - business will keep seeing the user_id in their bookings list, and via /export-data could surface old user records. Consider rewriting bookings.client_name / client_phone / client_email on the booking docs to '[Cuenta eliminada]' so businesses don't continue to display the redacted user's PII in their dashboards. Currently only the users collection is redacted.

routers/users.py: no check for OAuth-only users (no password_hash). The code does `if not user.get('password_hash') or not verify_password(...)` -> 401, so an OAuth user can NEVER self-delete via this endpoint. Either accept a password-reset flow first or expose a Google-confirm path.

routers/users.py: success email content is Spanish-only and template name is 'account_deleted'; ensure the email template exists in services/email or templates dir; otherwise it silently logs a warning and the user gets no confirmation.
test_credentials: JWTs minted directly with HS256 using JWT_SECRET from /app/backend/.env. Each test creates an isolated user with bcrypt password hash, prefix TEST_F10D_<RUN>_<rand>. No shared seed.
seed_data_creation: Per-test seed: users with bcrypt'd password 'Secret#123', plus per-test-specific docs in bookings (status confirmed/pending/completed), user_wallets (balance_mxn=0/50.50), payment_transactions, user_favorites, notifications, businesses. All cleaned up at module fixture teardown across users/businesses/bookings/payment_transactions/notifications/user_favorites/user_wallets/audit_logs by id-prefix OR user_id/business_id/actor_id/target_id/email prefix (including 'deleted_TEST_F10D_*@bookvia.deleted' redacted emails).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_fase10d_arco_rco.py is fully isolated per-test (each test creates its own user). The login-after-delete test exercises POST /api/auth/login (NOT unified-login) - if main agent reroutes the dashboard to unified-login post-delete behavior, add a parallel assertion against /api/auth/unified-login. test_delete_business_owner_blocked seeds a business doc but does NOT verify password change for the business; only the user-side delete is gated. Note Resend is MOCKED in this env - if the email template 'account_deleted' is added later, no test will fail (best-effort). All bcrypt validations use the same library version as core/security.py.
